content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

IBM watsonx Orchestrate exposes a REST API that lets you integrate AI agents directly into your applications and workflows. Whether you are building an internal automation pipeline or a customer-facing product, the API gives you programmatic access to agent orchestration, streaming responses, and an OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint.
This guide walks through the four essential operations you need to get up and running: obtaining an authentication token, listing your agents, sending a message with streaming, and calling the chat completions endpoint. All examples are shown in both cURL and Python.
Prerequisites
Set these environment variables once and every snippet below will work without modification.
export IBM_API_KEY="your_api_key_here"
export WXO_REGION="your_region"
export WXO_INSTANCE_ID="your_instance_id"Step 1 : Obtain an IAM Access Token
POST https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token
All API calls are authenticated with a short-lived IBM IAM bearer token. Exchange your API key for a token before making any other call. Tokens are valid for 3600 seconds (1hr) and must be refreshed periodically.
curl --location 'https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=urn:ibm:params:oauth:grant-type:apikey' \
--data-urlencode 'apikey=IBM_API_KEY'import requests, osresponse = requests.post(
"https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token",
headers={"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"},
data={
"grant_type": "urn:ibm:params:oauth:grant-type:apikey",
"apikey": os.getenv("IBM_API_KEY")
}
)
print(response.json())
{
"access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUz......",
"refresh_token": "not_supported",
"ims_user_id": 15031254,
"token_type": "Bearer",
"expires_in": 3600,
"expiration": 1774596742,
"scope": "ibm openid"
}Note: The response includes access_token, expires_in (3600), and token_type: Bearer. Cache and reuse the token across calls until it expires.
Step 2 : List Available Agents
GET /instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/agents
Retrieve all agents provisioned in your instance. The id field from this response is required for all subsequent calls.
curl 'https://api.$WXO_REGION.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com/instances/$WXO_INSTANCE_ID/v1/orchestrate/agents' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'region = os.getenv("WXO_REGION")
instance_id = os.getenv("WXO_INSTANCE_ID")response = requests.get(
f"https://api.{region}.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com"
f"/instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/agents",
headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
)
print(response.json())
[
{
"id": "4ba981cb...",
"tenant_id": "b319196991014918aeee542a3527dbd7...",
"name": "VoiceAgentTest_95874q",
"description": "testing voice agent",
"instructions": "",
"tools": [],
"collaborators": [],
"knowledge_base": [],
"llm": "groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b",
"style": "default",
"supported_apps": [],
"created_by": "IBMid-696000C5O6",
"created_on": "2026-03-20T06:22:49.593367Z",
"updated_by": "IBMid-696000C5O6",
"updated_at": "2026-03-25T08:33:10.818168Z",
"deleted_by": null,
"deleted_at": null,
"environments": [],
...
}
]Note: Each agent object includes its id, name, llm model binding, tools, collaborators, and audit timestamps. Store the id of the agent you want to interact with.
Step 3 : Send a Message with Streaming
POST /instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/runs?stream=true
The /runs endpoint starts an agent run and streams server-sent events back to the client — ideal for real-time UIs and long-running tasks.
curl 'https://api.$WXO_REGION.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com/instances/$WXO_INSTANCE_ID/v1/orchestrate/runs?stream=true&stream_timeout=120000&multiple_content=true' \
--header 'IAM-API-KEY: IBM_API_KEY' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{
"message": { "role": "user", "content": "Hello, how can you help me?" },
"agent_id": "YOUR_AGENT_ID"
}'response = requests.post(
f"https://api.{region}.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com"
f"/instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/runs",
params={"stream": "true", "stream_timeout": "120000", "multiple_content": "true"},
headers={
"IAM-API-KEY": os.getenv("IBM_API_KEY"),
"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
"Content-Type": "application/json"
},
json={
"message": {"role": "user", "content": "Hello, how can you help me?"},
"agent_id": agent_id
},
stream=True
)
response.raise_for_status()for line in response.iter_lines():
if line:
print(line) # process each SSE event
Raw: b'{"id": "177459...", "event": "run.started", "data": {"run_id": "6001dfb7...", "agent_id": "4ba981cb...", "thread_id": "f05f080a..."}}'
Raw: b'{"id": "177459...", "event": "message.started", "data": {"thread_id": "f05f080a...", "run_id": "6001dfb7..."}}'Event sequence: run.started → message.started → _…chunks…_ → run.completed. Each event carries a run_id and thread_id for correlating messages across a conversation.
Step 4 : Chat Completions (optional)
POST /instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/{agent_id}/chat/completions
For applications already built on the OpenAI Chat Completions interface, watsonx Orchestrate provides a drop-in compatible endpoint per agent — no client library changes required.
curl 'https://api.$WXO_REGION.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com/instances/$WXO_INSTANCE_ID/v1/orchestrate/$WXO_AGENT_ID/chat/completions' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{
"messages": [{ "role": "human", "content": "What services do you offer?" }],
"model": "open-ai/gpt-oss-120b",
"stream": false
}'response = requests.post(
f"https://api.{region}.watson-orchestrate.cloud.ibm.com"
f"/instances/{instance_id}/v1/orchestrate/{agent_id}/chat/completions",
headers={
"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
"Content-Type": "application/json"
},
json={
"messages": [{"role": "human", "content": "What services do you offer?"}],
"model": "open-ai/gpt-oss-120b",
"stream": False
}
)print(response.json())
{
"id": "chatcmpl-abc123",
"object": "chat.completion",
"created": 1234567890,
"model": "open-ai/gpt-oss-120b",
"choices": [
{
"index": 0,
"message": {
"role": "assistant",
"content": "We offer a wide range of services including..."
},
"finish_reason": "stop"
}
],
...
}Note: The response mirrors the OpenAI ChatCompletion schema, choices[0].message.content holds the reply and finish_reason indicates why generation stopped.
What’s Next
With authentication, agent discovery, and two interaction patterns in hand, you have everything required to build production-grade integrations on top of watsonx Orchestrate. From here, explore tool-calling to connect agents to external APIs, multi-agent collaboration, and context management to maintain conversation state across turns.
